priority_basis: The canonical queue contains no active frontier_research item, and R-024 explicitly left browser storage adaptation, tab races, bfcache integration, and production durability unresolved.
action: Convert the persistent acceptance ledger into a browser-appropriate IndexedDB transaction contract.
finding: IndexedDB overlapping readwrite transactions provide the browser-native serialization boundary. Duplicate detection, retention cleanup, insertion, and eviction belong in one transaction, and acceptance must not be reported until the complete event. Strict durability is only a user-agent hint.
verification: 5 deterministic Node tests passed; committed source and evidence require real-browser verification before production acceptance.
handoff: Vercel Studio should integrate R-023 validation with this IndexedDB consumer and add a Playwright or Web Platform Test using two same-origin pages. Verify exactly one complete event, bfcache behavior, upgrade blocking, quota failure, and private-browsing behavior.
